title: NWS Daily Climate Report (CLI)
description: Daily high/low temperature from NWS Climate Reports. THIS IS THE KALSHI SETTLEMENT SOURCE. One record per (station, date). Report type determines priority for upserts.

observation_date.description: Date of climate observation (YYYY-MM-DD). Note: during DST, NWS reports in local standard time, so the observation window is 1:00 AM to 12:59 AM next day, NOT midnight-to-midnight.

high_temp_f.description: Daily high temperature in Fahrenheit. SETTLEMENT FIELD for Kalshi HIGH_TEMP markets. Must be > low_temp_f when both present.

low_temp_f.description: Daily low temperature in Fahrenheit. SETTLEMENT FIELD for Kalshi LOW_TEMP markets. Must be < high_temp_f when both present.

report_type.description: Report priority. final (3.0) > ncei_final (2.5) > correction (2.0) > preliminary (1.0) > estimated (0.0). Only overwrite existing row if new priority > existing (strict). First-seen wins at equal priority, preserving the overnight final. report_type_priority is a parquet-only derived column (float64), NOT in this schema.

product_id.description: NWS product identifier. Format: YYYYMMDDHHNN-STATION-PRODUCT-TYPE. E.g., '202603150620-KOKX-CDUS41-CLINYC'. Used to extract issued_at and infer report_type.

issued_at.description: When the report was issued (RFC3339). Extracted from product_id timestamp.

$comment: BUSINESS RULES: (1) high_temp_f > low_temp_f when both present. (2) Report type priority governs upserts with strict > (not >=): never downgrade from 'final' to 'preliminary', and second 'final' for same date is dropped (first-seen wins). (3) Kalshi settles on the 'final' CLI report as displayed in NOWData at 10:00 AM ET the day after observation. Per NHIGH contract: 'Subsequent corrections, revisions, or quality control adjustments made after the Expiration time are not accounted for.' (4) CLI overnight final issued ~04:00-10:00 UTC (midnight-5am ET) next day. This is THE settlement value.
